title: Travel & Directions
shortSummary: Get around with confidence — ask for and understand directions, navigate public transport, book accommodation, and handle airport and border situations. Essential survival Spanish for any traveller.
whyItMatters: If you travel to a Spanish-speaking country, getting around is your most immediate need. Asking for directions, reading signs, understanding transport announcements, and checking into a hotel — these are non-negotiable skills.
coreConcepts: Direction vocabulary: derecha, izquierda, recto, cerca, lejos
Asking for directions: ¿Dónde está...? / ¿Cómo llego a...?
Transport: autobús, metro, tren, taxi, avión
At the airport: vuelo, puerta, equipaje, pasaporte
Reading signs and maps in Spanish
Prepositions of place: en, entre, al lado de, enfrente de
